D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Seasonal Colour Affect — Daylight, SAD, and Bright-Light Therapy

Latitude → daylight hours → serotonin/melatonin → Seasonal Affective Disorder; 10,000-lux light boxes weighted to blue 460 nm peak alleviate symptoms in ~80% of responders

Light-sensitive cells in the retina (ipRGCs with melanopsin) peak near 480 nm — they suppress melatonin during daylight and shift the circadian clock. SAD is ~5-10× more common above 50° latitude. Bright-light therapy at 10,000 lux for 30 min within 1 hr of waking is first-line treatment.

Slide month — Northern-hemisphere daylight + SAD-risk model.
